Welcome back everybody. Do you guys like that idea of having the groups like that? We saw you guys like planning Vegas trips and everything and it's going to be awesome. So I appreciate those guys are going to be helping out and being team leaders. And what the game plan kind of is, I'm going to have a meeting with those guys next week to kind of plan out some ideas behind how it's going to work and we're going to try it out for a month and see how it works, get some feedback, tweak it change and we'll just keep working. And I think it's going to be a big integral piece to making sure that, the worst thing I hate is doing an event like this, everything gets exciting, it's fired up and then you leave and then nothing happens. I want to make sure that we don't have that. I want to make sure that the goals and the dreams and things you guys are building here, that they keep moving forward and that every single time we get together, things are the next step and the next step and the next step. So it's constant evergreen progression. Somebody's business card. All right. So the last thing I want to talk about today, I have no idea how long this is going to go for, it might be like three or four minutes long, it might be an hour, but after this we're going to be done for the day. But I want to talk about a concept I think is really important to understand, because most of the internet people out there, we have the same issue, especially in the internet marketing world, but I see it bleed into all different industries. And the problem is that we put all this time and this effort behind creating a webinar or product or whatever it is, we get it up there and then there's this word that all these internet people, we always use called launching. We go and we launch this webinar, we launch this product. And launch is like, it's such a bad word because a launch, the reason I like the word launch is because with a launch there's a beginning, and then usually there's like an end of a launch. And the first time I really started thinking about this, because I looked at my business for the first probably five or six years that I was doing this game, and our entire business was, we came out with the product, we did all this time and effort, we put things together, we hired copywriters and put all this effort and product, and we do a big huge launch and make a really quick a million, two million dollars, whatever it was really quick, and it'd be awesome and then like two weeks later, like nobody ever bought the product again. And we're like, okay, we had to build something again because nobody's gonna, we sold it and we have no other ways to sell it, so let's go make another product. We built another one and we had years where we were launching four or five products in a year, and it's like so much time and effort and energy, and the money would be good right here, but then it would disappear really fast. So as much as you do launch and like it didn't do very well, and it was just, it was this horrible cycle that just wore me out, and so I remember the first time that I felt like there was a light to the end of my tunnel, because I remember doing this for like three or four years, and I'm like, I don't wanna keep doing this forever, like this is, that's a really like hard way to grow and to run a business, and I have a friend named Mary Ellen Tribby, and she worked for two different companies, first she worked for a financial publishing newsletter called Weiss, anyone here, Weiss Publishers? Couple, alright, so she worked for Weiss, and they were doing, I think they were doing like $7 million a year, they hired Mary Ellen and she came in, and within 18 months she took them from $7 million a year to like $60 million a year, and then she left Weiss, and then she went over to Agora, and she got hired to a division early to rise, I think they were doing $3 or $4 million a year, she came in, within 18 months they were doing like $80 million a year, and so I heard this story, so I met her the first time, I'm like, so you're really going to take in companies from like this to this really fast, like what's, how does that work? Because my company's about there, I would love to be right there, like that would be really awesome, like how do you do that, right? And so we were talking about the concept, and we were sitting there for a while, and she said, you know what, you internet marketers, you guys are so smart and you're so dumb at the same time, she's like, you guys are amazing, because you go out there, and she's like, what you do is the equivalent of you want to create a play, right? She says, you go out there and you hire the best screenwriters in the world, and they write an amazing play, and then you hire the best actors and actresses, and you practice, and you get this play perfect, and you're so excited, everything's perfect, and so then you go to downtown Boise, Idaho, you set up a sign, you tell everyone, this Friday we're doing a play, and everyone comes, the whole city, it's a sell out, you do the play, it's executed perfectly, it's a smashing success, and then the next morning, you shut down the play, and you start writing your next play. And she's like, all I do for companies like yours, Russell, she's like, you guys don't have a play that was a smashing success in Boise, Idaho, I just take your play, and I take it on the road, I take it to Chicago and to LA and to all these other places, and she's like, that's how I take businesses from six or seven million dollars a year to 80 million dollars a year that fast, so I take your already converting offers, and we take them on the road. And when she started saying that, I was like, wow, that sounds a lot better than making another play, and then another play, and another play. And so I want you guys to understand that one good webinar, like we've been talking about, one good webinar, that can be your business forever, like it can feed you more than you could ever dream about for a long time, and obviously making other products and services is good, but I'd rather, instead of focusing so much on making new things and adding new things, it's like, how do we take this one that's working really, really good, and how do we take that on the road? Where else can we start taking it to? And so with most of us, the first place, like the initial presentation, the initial thing you're doing, like for us here in Boise, Idaho, your initial thing you're doing is Facebook, right? Because there it is, but Facebook, we've kind of found something as we've been doing stuff, it's nice because it's not like a little city like Boise, it's a big city, right? It's Chicago, or LA, or New York, so there's a lot of road there, but eventually you do run out of space, right? You keep running Facebook ads on Facebook, after a while you're going to hit most of your market, and you're going to get saturations, it's going to be hard to keep scaling it. Let's use some of the stuff we talked about earlier today, where you're generalizing your thing and making it bigger, but there's a lot of other ways to drive traffic, and so after you kind of mastered once, like what are other ways to take this on the road and to kind of start taking it in new directions? So I don't want you guys to get too far that way right now, I want you guys to master the Facebook stuff first, that's where it's going to be low hanging fruit, but then start thinking back in your head, like how do I take this on the road, what are other avenues and other places I can start, taking this to scale it bigger, right? And so that's what I want you guys to kind of think about as these things are happening. How many of you guys were here at the last event we did? What month was that, I don't even know, in September? How many of you guys are here in the September event? Okay, it's about half you guys. So in September event I spent a lot of time talking about this, and I haven't seen too many guys actually doing it, so I'm going to kind of, a lot of this will be a recap for some of you guys, but the legacy aren't doing it yet, so I want to kind of restress it. Do you remember when we were here, I talked a lot about the Dream 100? Dream 100. Okay, so for those who don't know, I know you know a lot about Dream 100. I learned this concept initially from Chet Homes, he passed away three or four years ago now I guess, and he wrote a book called Ultimate Sales Machine, and in that book he has a whole chapter called the Dream 100, where he talked about, when he was first coming out and he was trying to go, and he looked at the top Fortune 100 companies who wanted to go and attract his clients, and so he figured, I need to find, here's my Dream clients, I need to go and have an effective marketing plan to go and get these guys, and one at a time to start closing them, right? And so he went and he picked his Dream 100, and then he started doing where, he'd mail out a physical letter to these guys talking to him, and then two weeks later he called them on the phone, and then two weeks later he'd mail another letter to them, and usually he was doing lumpy mail and all these things, he'd mail them something, then two weeks later call them, and mail them something, and just relentlessly pursued these people, and within a year, year and a half, he had like 50 or 60 of the top, do you know the exact numbers? Yes. Do you want to share? Do you have a mic? Do you know where the handheld is, anybody? These are no, Stacey is one of Chet and Tony Robbins business coaches, she's been training, all the people that pay them like 50 or 60 grand, she does all the coaching for those people, so. Yes, so Chet started, he was in a magazine, Nitch, working for Charlie Munger, who was Warren Buffett's partner, and he noticed they were number 16, I think, out of 16 magazines serving that niche, so he was really frustrated because Chet didn't like to be last, and so what he decided to do is start to look at the other magazines, and he noticed that there were these other advertisers that were in the other magazines that weren't in the magazine that he was selling for, so he made a list of 167 of the top advertisers, and that's when he started doing the Dream 100 process, so he started mailing them something, he needed to just mail them something, he mailed them a lumpy object, so something that the secretary wouldn't open for them, the secretary would say, oh, there's something in this, I better give it to him, and it would get actually on the executive's desk, and then call and follow up. In the first five months, nothing, so he was doing this, doing this, doing this, no results, in the sixth month he got his first one, by the end of the year he got 29 of those 167 advertisers, but that first client that they got bought the biggest ad that they'd ever had in the history, and he doubled sales, I think it was three years in a row for Charlie Munger before he left, and Charlie had called him into the office and said, all right, Chet, what are you doing, and tell me it's legal, because it was just going crazy, so that's the key, too, is the Dream 100, when I coach people on that, is not to give up, because it's not an overnight success story, it's not gonna happen right away, but in six months, getting 29 of these guys on his advertisers, it's crazy, so don't give up. It's awesome, give a round of applause, that's awesome. Okay, so my question is, how does that relate to what you guys are doing? So I'll explain what we're doing right now, because maybe it'll illustrate for you guys. So we have built out our Dream 100, it's bigger than 100 people, but I know exactly who my dream clients are doing my funnel hacks webinar, and I know that any one of these people, if I do the webinar with them with their list, will make anywhere from $100,000 to $1 million, on each and every single one we do, right? So if I know that I land a deal, and I can make anywhere from $100,000 to $1 million in my pocket, how much effort and time and energy should I put to try to close each of those people? Okay, it's worth a lot, right? One good person in your webinar, we did the webinar with Mike Philsames, we did $120,000 in sales, and he did not send out the replay. He had sent out the replay, we'd done over 200 grand. So just that one deal, boom, $100,000 in my pocket, right? For all of you guys, there are deals like that, they're all over the place. And so you have to start identifying, like who is it, what are the people that have an email list that if I do a promotion with them, I can make a bunch of money? Who are the companies? Sometimes it's not just a person, it might be a company. And you gotta start identifying who those people are, because each one of those people is worth a lot of money. For example, when we first started doing the funnel hacks one, we had our list and we've been hitting people up and it's been slow, it usually kind of starts slow at first, then you get one person to say yes, and you get stats and numbers, then it's easier to get the next person, and the next person, and soon it changes. Like right now, it's kind of funny, you can ask Brent, like we've had, I can't even tell you how many speaking gays and people are like, beg me to come speak on stage, hey come do funnel hacks, come do funnel hacks. And like half of my apps, they know it, because they're all happening when my wife's having a baby, so we keep turning these down. But like, as soon as you have a hot webinar, like it gets really, really easy to start promoting it and scaling it. I was telling Liz this when we've had our initial console call, I said, as soon as you get your webinar done, this is what's gonna happen. You're gonna test it, we're gonna get some numbers, as soon as you have a webinar that converts, then you're gonna go and we're gonna take this on the road, and we're gonna go and there's webinar circuits out there. Just like, you think about when this new movie comes out, what happens, what's the process that Hollywood goes to market movies? What's that? Yeah, the press. So you look at like, so for example, my wrestling coach, they just did a whole video of his life story. Have you ever seen Foxcatcher yet? Hasn't come to Boys Anyway. That's my wrestling coach at BYU, is his, Mark Schultz is his name, and his brother was one that died. So they did a big movie about their whole life story. And then they got the people like, chatting, I don't see this guy's name, chatting, tating, can never say that guy's name. So he played my coach, Mark played my coach's Mark Garofalo, he's gonna have a hard name, Rufalo, so hard. Steve Carell played the guy that killed my coach's brother. And so you look at that movie, comes out, how do they market the thing? They're on the today's show all three of them, one at a time, then they're on Jimmy Fallon, and they're on this one, and they're on this one, they're going through this media circuit, taking this thing on the road to promote the thing. Same thing happens with your webinar. Like when it's good, there's hundreds of people out there with lists in your target market. And you can come back to them and say, hey, I did this webinar, this is my stats, these are my numbers, it's not that hard to get them to want to promote. Like literally we have more webinars lined up for funnel hacks than I can even physically do. In fact, we're turning tons, like right now, we've told you they can't get at least 500 people to show up live on a webinar, we can't even do it because I don't have time to do it. Which is why we have the automated webinar ones, so people that have less can push to it. But when you have a webinar that converts, people start lining up and it's very easy contacting because they're there, they want money. They know that I promote this webinar, I'm gonna make X amount of dollars, okay? So for you guys that comes back to identifying who this is, because if you don't know who your Dream 100 is, like you can't go after them, right? And so how many of you guys have since last event, and I've been preaching this a lot, special about six months, how many of you guys have a Dream 100 list already built out? Raise them high, raise them high, you guys should be proud. All right, okay, for the rest of you guys, we gotta get this done, this is very, very important. One thing I love about, well, there's a lot of things, but Liz, for example, when I talked about Dream 100, like the next day, was it the next one day or two days later, sends me an Excel sheet, here's my Dream 100, what's next? Like, I'm not talking about doing this in the future, you guys, I'm talking about doing this right now, like you guys tonight should have your Dream 100 list done. So going in and if you don't know who it is yet, it's time to start finding out, like go into Facebook, like Mike was showing, and go start liking pages, so you can start seeing like, who are the people that are in your market that are selling stuff? Like you need to know who these people are, people are, you need to be on their list, you need to be seeing what their products are, and so you can start making the, okay, you know, I found this guy, he's selling the product similar, I went to ClickBank, here's a lot of people selling similar stuff, I went to Amazon and started building out this list, who are all the gurus, who are the people that are on TV, who are as many people as you can dream of, that are there and start building out your list? So our question was, in ClickBank, you go to like the business section, and most of it's like biz-op stuff at the top, and then Forex and some of the other ones, it's hard to dig down to like legit businesses, so unfortunately there's not a lot of legit businesses in ClickBank, like that market for whatever reason, like mostly legit businesses are like using Infusionsoft and things like that, like using external platforms, or more like the business opportunities are there, so knowing that, like I would go look at, like going into Google and typing in, like if you know that they're using Infusionsoft or Entrepreneur, like Googling stuff where you'd be able to find like order links to their products, you know, it's like business growth, expert Infusionsoft, and see if there's order forms coming, what their people are on the products, and kind of, so like it's not gonna be as easy for that specific to find who you want, going through Google and stuff like that, you're gonna find more people. Finding out the top bloggers is another key thing, especially for you business bloggers, or they're everywhere, there's tons of them. I mean you should know every blog that's in your vertical, or in like, not necessarily just have to be in your vertical, like, so here, let's just say you're, let me think for a second, like business blogs, right? So you have business blogs, so everything's business blog, then there's like personal development blogs, or personal development, and then there's other things that are similar, you know, even though you got a business product, like who are the personal development bloggers, because the personal development site can sell business stuff, you know what I'm saying? Because a lot of the personal development people are into business and vice versa, so it's kind of like, who are the main bloggers, again, you're Dream 100 category bloggers, and who are like the secondary ones that are similar, and who are those, and it comes down to you guys, like as an entrepreneur, I'm not huge as big on, on having you guys learn all the technical stuff, like how to run the ads, and how to set up the websites, and those kinds of things, I don't think those are like the energy that where most entrepreneurs should be focused in their time, I do think this is where it is, because it's hard for me to outsource someone and say, hey, go find all the best business blogs, they can find that, but at least in my mind, like that's kind of like, this is your research to find out like who are the people, because if you land one or two deals with the right people, like it can change your life forever, like it's just incredible, one or two little, little deals, little projects can do, but you gotta be able to map that, find out who those people are, and that's kind of the first step, okay? The second thing we talked about, we talked about last time here in Boise, the last event we were talking about was just the concept of digging your well before you're thirsty. So instead of, the big problem a lot of people have too, is the webinars done, they're like, okay, my webinar's done, go start calling people, start sending up deals to help me promote this thing, and help take this thing on the road, the problem is if the first time someone ever hears from you, you're calling them, asking them to promote something, it's probably the last time you'll ever talk to them. Yeah, Liz? Before I started this, I had no name, no list, like I had this little, well, it wasn't little, but this business in Christchurch, New Zealand, which had a couple of other clients around the world, but just pretty much a tiny wee business in New Zealand. So I had no name, but a lot of passion, right? And I used that passion a lot to grow, and to really get into the whole webinar, and I'm the person that he's talking about when he, oh, the webinar's really now what? And I'm like, well, it was like, with no list, I had no webinar, I had no stats, nothing to take, like to offer anyone, you know, to partner with, right? So I don't even know the guy's name, but I went to a webinar broker, we set up a meeting. We deleted his name from our memory, he's no longer. Okay, that's good, because I blocked him on Skype. Anyway, the guy was awful. Now I had no stats, but I was like full of steam, full of like enthusiasm, I'm ready to go, and I was like, oh, this is gonna change the world, cause this is gonna be the start. It was awful. That was one of those, probably one of those voxer messages that I was bawling my eyes at or something, and I, oh, I don't know, whatever, but honestly, the guy, he said to me, so what are your numbers? He was awful, what are your numbers? And I was like, oh, well, yeah, I'm looking at around 10%, cause I'm fudging my way through this, and I'm like, oh, yeah, around 10%, cause I'm like, well, you know, Russell said it would be about 10%, he goes, no, seriously, what are your numbers? I was like, and he was awful, so, you know, well, I'm gonna take 20% of that and 40% of this, and like, you know, well, actually, no, well, and it got to the point where he was just like, he was just plain awful, so it really made me understand that, you know, the whole dig, you're well before you're thirsty, like I was really, really, really thirsty. I had no well, I had nothing, I had no name, so this whole concept, like the way I got around this was to reach out to Mike and then start running that traffic to get the numbers to then start doing this, cause I've got stuff that I can actually leverage off the back of now, but I had nothing to begin with. Does that provide value? Yeah, definitely. Cool. So that's the big key, you guys, and that's why we talked about, like, getting this webinar, like, what she did, it was a mistake on my part, I thought this guy was way cooler than he is, obviously, and I introduced her and her, but obviously the first step is in doing this, is you've gotta kinda figure out your own thing, right? Know your numbers. Know yo numbers. So, and the best way to get those, cause it's hard, it's the chicken and the egg thing, like, I've got this webinar, how do I get someone to promote it? Well, it's hard when you don't have a list, you don't have your numbers or nothing like that, it's really, really hard, and that's what Facebook's so nice about it, it gives you a chance to do this, test it, get some sales, find out your numbers, you know what your conversion rates are, you know what your show, you have all those pieces done, cause now Liz has got a package, she can say, look, I've done this webinar 10 times, right now we're averaging this many, this many poor thing, this is my clothes rate, this is my thing, now she's got some stats, now she can go to somebody and come back here and say, hey, I've got this webinar, here's what it's doing, how would you like to be able to promote it? So that's the big part first, is really understanding your numbers. The next kinda piece of this is, is just kind of like, how do I say, it's like, I would say on an average week, we probably get anywhere on the low end from 10, on the high end, 20 or 30 emails a week, people contacting me asking me to promote something for them. Like they all go to Kelsey, she looks at them, she deletes them, doesn't even get to me, because people just ask that all the time, right? Like, if your first interaction with someone's like, hey, can you promote my stuff, the answer's almost always gonna be no, once in a blue moon you're getting lucky, but the majority of the time it's gonna be no, okay? But at the same time, if Mike feels the same text right now, he's like, hey, Russell, I got a thing, can you promote tomorrow? I'd be like, yeah, sure, man, no issue, what is it? Like, and that happened that fast. Okay, what's the difference between those two things? Not credibility, relationship, okay? Mike and I are best, I've known him for a long time, he's done stuff for me, I've done stuff for him, he's provided value for me outside of him actually promoting stuff, but just because we've built a good relationship, okay? When I have these Dream 100, long term I definitely want those guys to promote for me, like that's a big thing, but short term, I wanna become friends with them, okay? I want these guys to know who I am, I wanna be on their blogs, commenting, when they comment, when they're making Facebook posts, I wanna be telling jokes and be ahead of it, I wanna be in there, like, the people, like, I don't care how big of a name you're going after, if you're, every time they post something, you're in there commenting, they're gonna see your name over and over again, see your picture, you're gonna see stuff, it's funny because like, it's impossible to get emails, get emails from me, like, emails can't come to me, it's almost impossible, even you guys send me emails, I don't see them half the time, but like, on my personal Facebook, if I like say something on Facebook, and if you guys like it, like, I know that, my phone pops up and I'm like, oh, Mandy likes what I just said, I'm super proud of myself, right? Like, there's something different about that, where it's just like, it's really interesting, like, I bolded 211, how many of you guys knew I bolded 211 last week? Okay, everyone who commented, like, I saw all the comments, and I was like, Liz saw that, like, that's kinda cool, she knows I bolded 211, like, you know what I mean, it's stupid, but that's like the reality is that, the normal methods of communication are not gonna work with people, there's too many people trying to hit through them, they're trying to ask some stupid questions, but you become genuinely friends with someone, you get to know them, you like them, you comment on their stuff, that's how you start building relationships, and you see them in their face over, in their name over, and all of a sudden, you start building partnerships and friendships, and that's how this whole game is played, okay? I was like, before I started doing this whole thing, I thought business was like this technical weird thing, because I took a business class in high school and it sucked, it was like, it was just painful, and they were talking about all this stuff, and like, business, when it's all said and done, it's just relationships, like, who do you like, who likes you, and I mean, it works even like the Facebook level, like, if you know your account rep, you talk to them, if you know, you know what I mean, like, it's, I can't even tell you, it's all relationships, like, that's all that matters, and so, knowing that, like, you got to start digging your well today, okay, whether your webinar's done or not, it's time to start building relationships, building your Dream 100 list, and finding out who these people are, and relationships, sometimes it's easy, where you can go and blog and comment and follow them on Facebook and call them on the phone, set up a Skype meeting, whatever it is, but sometimes it's gonna be harder to get to people, but if you're consistent about it, you start building relationships, that's how this whole thing happens, so I can't stress enough, like, this stuff you guys all should have been started doing yesterday or six months ago, because now, that's the hard part, people are like, can my webinar's done, who can promote it, like, well, if you don't know the answer to that at this point, like, I don't know the answer, you got to be digging your well ahead of time, yeah. I know, it's funny, I have all that stuff in my office, still right now, the video's done, I just haven't actually put it out there. I'm playing, yeah, Dave's stuff, yeah, so, okay, so there's an idea, so one thing that we did to do that is Dave Asprey, who's one of the bigger people on my Dream 100, do I pronounce his name right this time? Where's Dave? Yeah, Asprey, right? Last time I said it wrong, he called me out on it. No, but I wanted to do it, he was on my Dream 100 list, and I was at his site, I was reading his blog, I was commenting and stuff, and I saw a thing that said, hire Dave for 500 bucks an hour, I'm like, sweet man, so I paid him 500 bucks, a week later, or a month later, we were on Skype, but doing Skype, hanging out, and guess what, now me and him are friends, and then, I'm like, hey, by the way, how can I help you, this is what I'm good at doing, and he's like, I don't know, maybe I'll connect with my Philly manager. Get on the phone with the Philly manager, Philly manager happened in about 108 split tests, it was super starstruck to talk to me, and I'm like, hey, we want to promote this thing for you, he got excited, shipped me out, $500 worth of stuff, and it was just this whole thing that happened because, boom, I identified first off who my person was, second off, I paid to build a relationship with him, sometimes you pay, sometimes you can do it organically, but I did that kind of a thing, and then now we've got a whole bunch of his products, and we're gonna do a cool giveaway with that, to help build a list, so there's, now I'll talk a little more about strategy here in a minute, but yeah, that's a cool thing, the other thing that's really cool, I told you guys last time, I think everybody here should start a podcast, how many of you guys have a podcast yet? Could be guys on the list, what's that? The marketing in your car? And we have a new podcast coming out too, actually Natasha, by the way, your episode's coming up soon, so I recorded a bunch like a little while ago, I haven't had a chance to launch it, but the coolest thing about a podcast, after you've identified your dream 100, this is one of the best ways to have a chance to get to know these people at an intimate level, okay? Because if you recall any of these guys, I'm gonna say, hey, can I pick your brain for now, or guess what they're gonna say? No, hang up, right? But you call someone up and say, hey, I've got my own podcast, which in their mind is a platform for them to build their distribution, right? I've got my own podcast, I would love, my audience has been begging for me to interview you, you're one of the top three picks, can you be on my podcast? Almost everyone says yes, okay? I shared this story last time, and I think it's kind of cool, because who were watching Celebrity Apprentice? The new one just came back out again, it was, like, canceled, it's been like two or three years, I think, but it started again. But the last one prior to this season, who remembers who won it? No, Gary, if you see didn't win it, he was the worst. Who's this? This is, our Cineohall won it, okay, come on now. So our Cineohall, this is my favorite lessons I learned from the Apprentice. So our Cineohall is on it, they're doing this whole charity thing where they have to raise money, so all these dudes are on their phone dialing all their people, dialing for dollars and everything, and our Cineohall's over here, and you see all the people, like this dude made this much money, everything, and shows our Cineohall, and he can't get a single person to pick up the phone, right? He spends three days calling people, and every other celebrity raises a ton of money, except for our Cineohall, he's got zero dollars raised, and he's all depressed and stuff, and they said this line that I thought was really, really cool. He said, when I had my own talk show, everybody answered my phone calls, okay? When I had my own platform, everybody answered his phone calls, because what do people want, what do celebrities want? They want a platform, they want to be able to show off how cool they are, and our Cineohall used to be able to facilitate that, when that platform was gone, he was a nobody, okay? So for you guys, you gotta reverse engineer that, okay? If you have your own platform, people are gonna pick up your call. So if you've identified your Dream 100, you launch a podcast and you start going after them, something you can get interviews with people you never could have before, you have a chance to talk to them for 30 minutes, or for an hour, pick their brain, get ideas from them, but you build a relationship with them as well, and because you're putting them up on a platform, on a pedestal, you're providing value to them, in a way that they, that's really cool and really, really unique. Does that make sense, you guys? Okay, so everyone should have a podcast. If you don't have one yet, like, it's time to start. I've got my little one I do on my car rides in every single day, but we're launching an actual dot com secrets one, that'll be more focused on that, of just going out to my Dream 100 and just doing calls with people and bringing them in, but it's something that's not hard to do. There's a couple of little technical things, but it's not that confusing. I can maybe put together a guide, I can have my brother put together a guide for you guys if you want, it shows you like, here's the three steps, but it's not that hard to get a podcast up. Excuse me, and then you just do it, okay? So doing a podcast is a great way to start getting to know people, build relationships, and then number three, just be cool with these guys. Like, that's just, that's the thing like, don't try, don't ruin it by trying to get them to promote and to promote and promote, like that's like the worst thing to do. It's just like being cool about it, like, hey, here's how I can help, here's how, how can I help you in like, trying to serve people? But then you have to be, you have to, there's a time when you have to ask, and you have to be willing to ask. Okay, I've had friends who have been, friends of mine for a long time that never asked me to promote, and like, and because of that, I never do, okay? They've done a good job digging the well, digging the well, digging the well, but there has to come a time when you're, and this is something that's scary, especially when it's a personal relationship, I still struggle with it a lot. When you actually come to that person at that time and say, hey, we've been friends for a long time, we've been doing stuff together, will you do this for me? And it's like so hard sometimes, sometimes they say no, but you do have to be willing after you've built the relationships to ask, because if you don't ask, it's never gonna happen. And so if you have stuff like this, it makes it a lot better. And the cool thing too is ask, be cool. He's very technical. Tim Ferriss, I read a thing about him when he launched the four hour work week, and he said that what happened was he kind of went through the same process, a little bit different, but conceptually the same. He said that he was, he started writing this book and said, you know, I'm gonna go and just get to know people, like, because I know someday I'm gonna want these guys to promote my book, but I don't know when, but someday they will. So he went out there and he started, he never said it in these terms, but he built out his Dream 100 list of bloggers, very influential bloggers. He just went and started commenting, calling them up on the phone, getting to know these guys, building relationships. And he said he became really good friends with all these people and they were cool. And then after a while everyone's like, so what do you do for a living? Like we see you on Facebook, like what do you do? And he's like, oh, I'm just trying to book. They're like, really? He's like, yeah, like what's it about? Like, oh, it's not done yet. I'll let you know when it gets closer. And he kept kind of blowing it off. And he's building relationships and stuff. And then after a year or two years, however long it took him to get the book done, he's messaged these guys and like, hey, so I'm kind of like booked, I just want to see if you want to read a copy of it. They're like, sure, I'll check it out. Like, we're friends, you wrote a book, like of course I'll check it out. So he starts sending this book out to people. And luckily it was a really cool book. And so all these Dream 100 had this book. And then he's like, hey, I'm gonna launch this book. If you guys like it, you mind writing a review about it. And they're like, oh, sure. And all of a sudden, launch day came. He had the 100 most influential bloggers in the world post blog posts. And boom, overnight came out of New York Times bestseller. Got instant PR publicity all around the country and has built the platform he's on now, which is just insane. There's few people that have built an online platform like his and it all started with this process of building out this Dream 100, building relationships, digging your well before you're thirsty and then boom, releasing it like that. Then the Twitter, just hitting the where they're at. Cause everyone's got their favorite communication platform, right? You figure out like where they're at, getting it on there and serving them. So that's the kind of concept you guys, I want you guys to keep, I keep talking about this, but I want you guys to start doing it. I want you guys tonight to start building your Dream 100 list. After you have that, start building relationships, starting a podcast, following all these guys on Facebook, getting them on Twitter, whatever platform you're in, get on those platforms, comment and keep up with them. And the most important thing I think, I mean not most important, one of the most important things is it's important that when you do have a chance, cause every once in a while you're gonna have a chance to step up and you're gonna have a chance to interview someone or talk to them on the phone and you need to be prepared for that moment. One of the worst things that has happened a couple times to me is somebody will like, like somehow if someone gets to my gatekeeper, somebody get a hold of me and they wanna talk to me and somehow it happens, right? We set up the column, we start talking and really quickly you can tell if somebody's been paying attention. I got people before, I've had people message me like, oh yeah, so it's cool, I saw your project, some project I done like three or four years earlier and I'm like, that's weird, like how would they know, like how would that be the thing they talk about and they start talking to me and I'm like, like so what are you working on? Are you giving me projects? I'm like, oh, you heard about ClickFunnels? They're like, no, what's that? I'm like, okay, if you don't know about ClickFunnels, like that's all I've talked about for the last year of my life, like obviously you're not paying attention, like you know, and instantly I lose all credit, that person's all credibility that fast. So when you have that opportunity, it's like the reason why I'm doing this, why you're following on Facebook and you're reading what they're saying, you're paying attention is because sometimes you have a chance to talk to that person and if you don't know what matters to them, it's you're gonna lose the deal really quickly. If you know what matters to them, it'll change everything. One of the best examples of this story is one of my close friends, Stu McLaren, when he was first launching his business, he was doing some seminars and he invited David Fry to come speak at his event and Stu is a smart dude, he's been on David's email list and reads all the emails he comes out with and one day he called David up and was like, hey, I'd love to have you come speak at my event and David said yes and then Stu said, hey, would you be willing to promote the event to your list? And Dave's like, I don't do that, I'm sorry. And she's like, that's okay, no worries. And then at the end of it, Stu's like, oh, by the way, how's Ingrid doing? I read about blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, whatever. And David's like, wow, I sent an email out three weeks ago about this random thing and not only did Stu listen to that, he remembered it and he brought it up and asked me about it later. And anyway, David hung up the phone and a few minutes later he called back and said, you know what, Stu, he's like, I've never done this in my entire life, I've never promoted an event, but I'm gonna promote an event for you. And anyway, from that one promotion, the time David's list was like 50 or 60,000, like really legitimate business owners, and he helped fill up Stu's entire event, made him tons of money because Stu was listening. Okay, there's a guy that I'm trying to, one of the people in my Dream 100 list, he's in the real estate market, and it's a really super cool guy, and I've been trying to build my dig the well with him and get to know him a lot. And I read one of his blog posts he was talking about, he had this really bad back surgery and back pain, all the stuff that happened to him. And so last week I had the blue eyes, I was like, hey man, just check and see, is your back doing any better, how are things going? And I remember he went back like an hour later, he's like, wow, how in the world did you know my back was having issues? He's like, that's really cool to even notice that, and yeah, actually this is what happened, he told me his whole story about it, and it just built that relationship that much deeper because I knew what he cared about. Okay, and so I want you guys to understand that, I don't get big deals because I'm Russell Brunson or anything, I get big deals because I care about people and at a different level. In January, we've got three, or excuse me, in February we've got three big people promoting ClickFunnels, I think between those three promotions we'll do over a million dollars, and all three of those are people that I have served, I've given to, I've never asked them to promote anything in return, because I knew that someday I was gonna have something and all he did is like message them up and said, hey, I got this thing, would you be willing to do it? And all three of them said yes, okay, because I've been digging my well. And so that's the key, you guys, is like that stuff starts now, don't delay that for a week for now or six months for now or a year for now, like start this stuff now because I promise you, as soon as you get an offer and a webinar that works, you're gonna want to take that on the road, and if you've done that, you've got your numbers down, you've got relationships, you've got people you know, like it makes that job so much easier, it makes it faster and faster to scale things up. Yes, Kay, you want me to give you guys my super good secret to success? Kay, how many of you guys have ever seen the movie Never Been Kissed by Drew Barrymore? How many women have seen it? Any of the men seen it? Not any men have seen it, like you guys, if you haven't seen this, you're screwed, this is a very important marketing lesson. Kay, so here's the concept, we should watch this tonight. Kay, so Drew Barrymore, she's like a super nerd in high school, has no friends, she's like on the math clubs and stuff, and Never Been Kissed, she graduates, and then after she graduates, she goes and she's a job as a journalist, and she's a very successful journalist, she's still Never Been Kissed her whole life, right? And one day her boss says, I need you to go back to high school and find out what the kids are actually talking about today and do this whole like undercover journal thing. She's like, I hated high school, I didn't know anybody, nobody liked me and this can be really hard, and so she agrees to it, she goes back to high school and she comes in as a fake high school kid, and she tries to come in within like five seconds, she slips back into her old friends, and she's in the math club and all these things, and she's bringing the stories back to her boss, and he's like, I don't care what the math club kids are doing, I need to know what the cool kids, like this is what the articles are about are the cool kids, and so she's all depressed because she can't get any kind of traction, right? So then comes along her brother, who's played by David Arquette, and he's seen his sister going back in school and having all this fun, and he's like, crap, high school was the greatest time of my life, like I was the coolest kid in the world, and I was the athlete, everything was fun, and so he sneaks into school and pretends like he's a kid, he gets into school, I promise you guys there's a point to the story, so he gets into school and he comes up to Drew Barrymore, and he's like, and to his sister in the movie, and he's like, and something, and he's like, I'm gonna be the coolest kid in this whole school again, and she's like, no, it's not that easy to be the cool kid, and he's like, watch this, and so he goes over and he's in the cafeteria, and he gets this huge bucket of coleslaw, yeah, and he's sitting there, and he's eating the whole bucket like this, and everyone's cheering, and he eats the whole bucket by himself, and he's suddenly the most popular kid in town, and so she's all impressed, like, man, this guy's awesome, and I'm a loser, and then this is where the connection comes, so he's like, check this out, he's like, I'm gonna make you popular that fast, and she's like, well, how are you gonna do that? And so he came up to all the hot Q girls in the room, and he's like, hey, see that girl over there? Man, she dumped me, she broke my heart, she's the most amazing woman I've ever met, and so I tell everybody this, and all of a sudden, the one cool, he became the cool kid, and when the one cool kid told everyone that she was cool, what happened? Instantly, she became cool, right? Okay, so the lesson is, you don't have to get 100 people to all love you, right? If you come in here, and you get one cool kid to take your risk, and you come in, here's my numbers, we have a webinar, you want a cool kid to do it, that person promotes it, then all of a sudden, when that one cool kid thinks you're cool, you come back and say, hey, the webinar's worked, who else do you think it's gonna be good for? Okay, for example, we did the webinar with Mike Phil's name, so we pay our fillings out, 40% on the webinar sale, so Mike did the webinar, and after it was done, he crushed it, and I said, hey Mike, did you like the webinar? He said, yeah, it was awesome, everything's great, man, cool, so we'll give you 10% of anyone you broke this webinar out to. Mike instantly sent an email to all of his partners, and saying, hey, we're also gonna have a webinar, we should have $120,000 in sales, it was a clean offer, people loved it, if you wanna do it, here's the message, contact Brent, and all of a sudden, the next day, we had 20 people lining up, hey, do you wanna do the webinar, do the webinar? Mike got 10% commission, they get 40%, and then that happens, and then every webinar we do, next thing we do, after we do the webinar, we send them out stakes, just tell them we love them, and then we're like, hey, by the way, do you have anyone else? And so that's kinda how we start growing it faster, like that, does that help? So I told you that was a good movie, now you guys wanna go see it, huh? That's definitely it, so you guys, this is the long-term strategy, but like I said, the goal is you gotta get one or two cool kids to think you're cool, and then you're in, that's the thing, if you look at, so was I Drew Barrymore, or were you Drew Barrymore? No, no, I'm not. I'm just kidding. So he's saying, so I was speaking at a Dan Kenney event, and then his partner came up to me and met me, and we ended up creating Docum Series Local together, we launched it, and blew that whole thing up, and yeah, great. So that's the concept, you guys, is this is the long-term play, and again, understanding that you don't have to get 100 people to love you, but starting with one or two, and then using that to scale and to grow among the other ones. One more good example, I kinda share this, I thought was interesting, and we hear about Telman Knudsen, so Telman was this big, he shifted to more like the hypnosis, personal development space, but when I first got started, about two or three years into my business, he called me one day, and he's like, hey Russell, I got this idea, and this is how it's gonna work, and he pitched me this idea, and I thought it was stupid, I'm like nope, sorry, bad idea, and I hung up, and then, I'm gonna notice to me, I didn't think, I thought everyone would've told me, but he called 49 people, I believe that's the number, 49 people, and asked them to do the same deal that he asked me to do, 49 people told them no, just like me, nope, bad idea, nope, bad idea, calling, calling, calling, I think it was the 49th person he called, was Kevin Wilkie and Matt Gill from Nitro Marketing, and Kevin and Matt said yes, and Telman's like sweet, I got one yes, and then he called the next guy and said, hey Kevin and Matt, are you in? And that person said yes, and called the next person, hey Kevin, Matt, and so on and so on, and the next 30 people he called all said yes, and it's pretty powerful having one person, we did this really big promotion a few years ago, called the online market of the year, we were trying to get 80 people to all promote this event we did, and so the very first person we got on board was Dan Kennedy, I wrote them a check for $25,000 for him to say yes, and then I called everyone else and said, hey Dan Kennedy isn't on the still, you wanna be in? And the guy's like, I don't know what it is, but I'm in, hey, Dan Kennedy and so on, so you hit, and all of a sudden they started lining up, and started knocking them down really, really fast, so those are some strategies we've done, some strategies help kind of fast track your success. Okay, I got one more kind of cool thing I wanna share with you guys, related to traffic, it's not really Dream 100, but something that, again, I want you guys thinking about this, and doing this from day one as you're growing right now, the other thing I want you guys to think about, and this is something I have not been doing, so I'm not a good example of this yet, but I'm repenting of my ways, and we are starting to do this next week, you will see it actually launching with us, and I think it's important enough to talk to you about it, because it's important strategy. So if you go to page number 76 here, I made a little cool graphic called inbound hacks. Are you guys following like the whole inbound thing that people are talking about right now online, couple of you guys? So inbound marketing, conceptually I hate the concept of this, people like, so there's actually, there's two worlds, right? So there's outbound marketers, which is people traditionally like me, where we put things out, we're pushing things out, right? And there's inbound guys, and the inbound guys are like, all you have to do is post cool content, and people will give you money, okay? And I don't necessarily agree with these guys at 100%, but what's happening is that the world's changing in a way where this is becoming more and more what people want. For example, a lot of the ad networks right now, and I think Facebook is shifting more towards this as well, but they don't want you driving traffic directly to a video sales letter or to a squeeze page or to a webinar registration page. They want people on their platform to feel like they're getting value, right? So they want you pushing content to more content. So you have, let's say you're here on Facebook, here's Facebook, they want you to have like an article here, you click on the article, it goes over to your blog, and on your blog then you can push somebody to go sell them something, right? And so this is what inbound marketing is, is this content piece in the middle, and this is what ad networks and things want more and more. And I think this can continue to go that way, okay? They're gonna like us as marketers less and less, and they're gonna want just more of us promoting content. Like that's where, and that's what we look at at Facebook's right now, they're making you now, they used to just, you post content, they show it to all your fans, we're now, you post content, you gotta pay to show it to all your fans. That's the direction everything's going. So it's important to understand that right now, because we're not there now, but within the next six months, a year, two, and beyond, this is the future, okay? And so understanding that, like we've gotta become good at this because that's where everything is getting pushed to, okay? They're trying to push us out, and they're trying to push this in, okay? So I've been looking at this for the last six months, and so really in depth, trying to see like, what are people doing, what's working, what's not working, what are the core pieces behind it? And one of my favorite persons that I've kind of been, have been studying is a guy named Neil Patel. Any of you guys have been studying Neil Patel, or seen his stuff at all? So Neil's got two blogs, he's built up multiple, multi-million dollar businesses, and if you look in here on page 77, and then page 80, I had a monocle in my office, I said, go to Neil's two blogs, and describe the most recent 100 headlines of his most recent blog posts. So these are the most recent 100 headlines of both of his two blogs, just so you kind of get an idea of what I'm talking about. But what Neil does is every single morning, he wakes up and he writes a blog post for Quick Sprout, which is his personal blog, and then he writes a blog post for Kiss Metrics, which is his software company. And every day he does a blog post, and he puts it out there, right? Makes a blog post, then takes that blog post, posts it on Facebook, and drives some ads to push that blog post, to pay some traffic to push this blog post out to his followers, right? And that's his method, that's his core, I mean he does SCO and stuff like that, but his core advertising method is this, a blog post a day, every single day, and he's putting it out there, okay? And so what's cool is if you look at his headlines, like they're all the kind of things that if you were on Facebook, you'd probably read, right? Or you click on it, like what is that thing, right? I'll read some of them. Let's see, how to keep your readers longer on your blog. Don't blog unless you use these 11 tools. How Google crawls, indexes, and index webpages. 100 lessons learned from 10 years of blogging. How a Ferrari made me a million bucks. The 10 key elements of high quality websites. How to spend $6,000 on clothes that made me $692,000. How to reach 100,000 visitors with these seven rules. Okay, so these headlines are all like, they're all like very specific, you know? It's that like the one thing here, the 100 things here that, so they're like daily, a thing like that. And each blog post is like, so they're not super long, they're almost like an email, talking about one or two concepts, and then pushing back to his core offers. Okay, it's a consistent thing, right? And so you look at our business, what we're doing is, and I've never in the 10 plus years of me doing this, ever had a blog or ever been consistent with the blog, but coming out next week, we're gonna be launching our blog where, you'll see almost daily, well, that's not gonna be true actually. A couple times a week, I'm gonna be writing blog posts with this goal, where I write a blog post, I post it on my blog, after it's done, then this is where the inbound and the outbound kind of marry. The blog post is done, I go into Facebook, I've got my audience is set up, and then I push that blog post out to people, right? Where's the good blog post, someone's gonna see that on Facebook, they click on it, kind of my blog, they read, and then from there I push them over to either my opt-in box, my registration page, whatever my goal is with that thing, okay? And so I wanted you guys to start thinking that way as well. Now there's two kind of strategies with this, and some of you guys will gravitate towards one versus the other. The first strategy is someone like Neil, who likes to write everyday, where he writes a blog post every single day, I write a blog post, post it, write a blog post, post it. How many of you guys are big writers, or that seems really appealing to you? A couple of you guys, okay? I know for me that it scares me a little bit. The other method, and neither of these are right or wrong, they're just two different methods. The other one's kind of cool, I've been seeing Justin Brok do, and I talked to him about this, where he looks at blog posts as pre-content for what he's gonna sell, right? So he's got a webinar over here, he's gonna write a blog post, like pre-selling the webinar, that becomes the blog post, but he titles it in a way that's really attractive, kind of like Neil Patel's things. And so he says that, he used to tell me, he's like, I've got one blog post I wrote to pre-sell his one-on-one services, and he says, whenever I need new clients, he's like, the blog post is always on my blog, but he's like, whenever I need new clients, I go back to Facebook, and I start promoting that thing out there, I'll promote it, promote it, get a bunch of people in, and as soon as I got a bunch of people in, then I just turn it off, and then I'm just good. So I need more clients, I turn that blog post back on again, and I turn it off. Okay, so the other piece of this, I was talking to, how much of Jeff's in here, Sal Zinstein, but I was talking to him about this, a little bit through Voxer, with this strategy where, if you know like your core things you're trying to do, right, you're trying to get people to join your list, you're trying to get people maybe to buy a free plus shipping offer, you're trying to get people to register for your webinar, and so writing a blog post that's very specific about who you're trying to attract, if you write it, post it on Facebook, and send it out to the right people. Okay, so for example, what I might do is, I might write a blog post that says, seven ways that chiropractors can get more leads from their website, right? So I make a blog post, and this blog post, I'm only writing it once ever, but it's, my entire goal is to target chiropractors, right? Write that blog post, then I go to Facebook, we make a custom audience just for chiropractors, and boom, that's in their news feed every single day. So all the chiropractors see that, does that make sense? Okay, then the next blog post I write, I know I'm gonna be targeting network marketers in Vima, for example, right? So then I'm like, here's three things all Vima distributors are doing wrong, okay? And make a blog post very specific to that. Going to Facebook, target the Vima distributors, and I push that out. Now I've got like an anchor that's grabbing in all the Vima distributors, right? Then I go through, so each day for me, is I'm making very strategic posts, they're gonna grab different sections of Facebook and bring them into my world, okay? So that's how I'm gonna be using this, and that's kind of the strategy. Does that make any questions about that, how that kind of works? So that's kind of the inbound stuff, and I'm not gonna spend a lot of time on that, but I wanted you guys to start thinking about that. I am actually setting up a WordPress blog, for those who asked me about WordPress, even though I hate it. So we're setting up a WordPress blog, and so I will give you guys, for our next meeting, I'll have more results coming, be doing really consistently hard for a few months, and just kind of see what comes from it. But I think that it's worth it for all you guys to start that process now, to start that consistency, because that's where things are going, and I'm a big believer in like, if I see where things are going, I don't wanna show up there, like six months after the party started, like I wanna get my legs and my things in there ahead of time, and so like as I said, as we start testing and learning things, and kind of seeing what's happening, I'll share those things with you guys, but just conceptually I think it's smart for everyone to set aside a part of each of your week to do like blog posts like that, and I recommend going to Neil Patel's blogs, other blogs you like that you click on in your read, and subscribe so you can kind of see what they're happening. Every day Neil writes a blog post, sends an email, and I just, I never read any of the articles, I look at the headlines, because they're so good. I think about how easily you could take any of these headlines and transform it for your business, right? So his is, why 2015 might be your best year for internet marketing? Oh, why 2015 might be your best year for losing weight, right? 12 tips for increasing your copyrighting, click the rates on Twitter. 12 tips for increasing, you know whatever, you just kind of tweak it for your business, like his headlines are just their gold, and every single day you get a brand new one and deliver in your inbox, and you can take those, and kind of tweak them, and make them work. The other cool thing, and I'll just kind of mention this really quick, Christopher's working with me on this project, he created a really cool plugin for WordPress, that again, it's part of this test that we're gonna be doing, and I'm excited where, where basically somebody can become, it's hard to explain this without like, getting too deep into it. So right now, for example, my goal is I write a blog post, I go on Facebook, and I push it out there, right? But the next level of that is like, what if I could get other people to also be incentivized to take my blog post and to push it out there? How cool would that be, right? Or if I get my Dream 100, or if I get all my affiliates, every time I make a blog post, I get 1,000 people sharing it. So it's not just my efforts, but it's 100 people, or 1,000 people's efforts, right? So he created a whole amazing plugin that does that, where basically, you guys, or whoever comes to my blog, they join as an affiliate for my blog, they sign up for any of the products I recommend on my blog, they sign up for them, and then they get an affiliate link for every single blog post I do. I do a blog post, then they can go out there, post them on Facebook, push it to their group, whatever, and if someone clicks on it, comes back here, and they buy anything, they get paid off it. And so that's kind of another cool thing we're gonna be testing that just takes this inbound marketing concept and just sets it on fire. Yeah. Yeah, yeah, it's a cool plugin. He built it called Share Engine. So I said, we're one of his testers, we're gonna be kind of proving it out over the next three or four months and seeing, but it's pretty sweet. I'm excited for it. All right, so that's kind of some of the things again. I didn't go too deep in these yet, you guys, because again, I'm experimenting with some of the stuff, but I wanted you guys to kind of be aware of it, and I'll show you more results as we kind of go through. Yeah, for those of you guys who have covered it through your mark, we'll set up renewal information. But anyway, but I just want to say, you guys, like this last year, so we kind of decided to put together this whole, this Ignite and Inner Circle program. At first, like I kind of didn't want to do it when we were fighting against it, and we kind of like, do we do it, do we not do it? It's one of those things where it's hard because it takes a lot of time and effort, but for me personally, it's been probably the most rewarding year of my business, because when we designed this in a way where it wasn't just going to be like, you sign up for coaching and you have coaches and we kind of forget about you, I wanted to be personally involved with all of your guys' businesses, and I was scared at first, we were going to keep up with it and stuff, but it's been so fun, and you guys voxing me has been like, it's exciting, and every single time I get excited, I'm like, what's Liz going to say today, and what's Jason going to say? It's just so much fun for me, and so I appreciate you guys using that as a tool, and don't feel guilty about using it, like it's good for me, it keeps me on my toes, it helps me keep learning and thinking about things and finding resources, and it's just been fun and super rewarding for me, so I appreciate it, and I appreciate you guys allowing me to be in your businesses and helping and molding, and my goal is always to try to do the best we can, and we honestly do care about you and your successes, and I hope you guys feel that, and you believe that, we really do, and we are here for you guys, and if you have any issues or questions or concerns outside of this, you've got me on Voxer, you've got Brett and John calling ours, everything else, but I just want to say I appreciate you guys, it's fun seeing your guys' businesses evolve and change, and I'm just excited to see what the next year is going to do for all you guys, so everyone, give yourselves a round of applause, I'm excited. Thank you.
